page: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is a virtual server instance within the High Performance family. It features an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 shared vCPUs across 4 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz with 1 thread per core. The system is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 180 GB NVMe SSD. No dedicated GPU hardware is included, and the instance comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a mixture of performance characteristics. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2306.16, while multi-core CPU performance is average at 9232.12. However, memory performance is a notable bottleneck; uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks are poor, falling in the bottom 10% at 17.9 GB/sec and 12.56 GB/sec respectively. Similarly, PassMark memory scores and database operations are in the bottom 10%, and Redis SET operations are weak at 3.94 million operations per second.

The shared CPU allocation and poor uncached memory bandwidth present clear tradeoffs for memory-intensive or sustained multi-threaded workloads. Single-threaded tasks benefit from the strong single-core performance, but data-heavy applications like Redis or database operations perform poorly. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high-capacity NVMe SSD and a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio on a shared CPU model, making it a budget-friendly option for workloads that require fast local storage but have low memory-throughput demands. It is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight single-threaded applications, and development environments.
description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 180 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks, though its multi-core performance remains average. A key tradeoff is its memory subsystem; uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths, along with PassMark database operations, fall into the bottom 10% of benchmarks. Lacking a GPU, this server is qualitatively cost-effective for storage-reliant workloads with low memory-throughput demands. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and development environments where high-speed local storage is required but memory bandwidth is not a critical performance factor.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is a virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 180 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core CPU performance alongside limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 180 GB NVMe SSD for storage-focused workloads.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server pairing strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with high-speed local NVMe storage for budget-conscious workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC-Rome architecture with 4 shared vCPUs and 4 physical cores
8.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 180 GB NVMe SSD
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Reduced memory bandwidth and database performance under heavy loads
Well-suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic web serving
